commit.message:
Implement complete C++17 Text-to-Papercraft pipeline (#1)

* Initial plan

* Add complete Text-to-Papercraft C++17 pipeline

19 files implementing all pipeline stages:
- CMakeLists.txt: OpenMesh 9.0, Eigen3, OpenMP, nlohmann/json, stb via FetchContent
- src/config.h: Config struct with JSON/CLI loading, validation, pretty-print
- src/utils.h: header-only Timer, log(), log_metric(), ensure_dir(), deg2rad(), clamp()
- src/mesh_loader.h/.cpp: load/repair/BFS-component/stats/save/ortho-rasterise
- src/simplification.h/.cpp: dihedral fold detection + QEM decimater retry loop
- src/segmentation.h/.cpp: face adj matrix, normalised Laplacian spectral embedding,
  k-means++ Lloyd, elbow detection, ensure_disk_topology, PNG renders
- src/unfolding.h/.cpp: LSCM from scratch (Lévy 2002 complex weights, SimplicialLDLT),
  planar_projection PCA fallback, ARAP proxy, OpenMP parallel unfold
- src/sheet_generator.h/.cpp: scaling, boundary-edge tab extrusion, shelf packing,
  AABB SAT overlap detection, Bresenham sheet render
- src/metrics.h/.cpp: table print, JSON/TXT/PNG report output
- src/main.cpp: 6-stage wired pipeline with per-stage timers
- src/stb_impl.cpp: STB_IMAGE_WRITE_IMPLEMENTATION translation unit
- config.json, .gitignore

Verified: builds cleanly with GCC 13.3 / CMake 3.28 / Release;
full pipeline on test cube generates 9 output files correctly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix code review: Eigen coefficient access, lambda rename, structured binding

commit.message:
fix(display): remove broken PauseAndRun — use ReadKey or ContentPanelMenu (#1265) (#1266)

* fix(display): remove broken PauseAndRun — use ReadKey or ContentPanelMenu (#1265)

Removes the fundamentally broken PauseAndRun method that attempted to pause
Live rendering to call AnsiConsole.Prompt(). Spectre.Console's
DefaultExclusivityMode holds _running=1 for the entire Live.Start() callback
duration — blocking the render thread does NOT release the exclusivity lock.

Changes:
- Remove PauseAndRun method and associated pause/resume event signaling
- Fix ShowSkillTreeMenu to call AnsiConsole.Prompt directly when Live is not active
- Remove _pauseDepth, _pauseLiveEvent, _liveIsPausedEvent, _resumeLiveEvent fields
- Simplify Live render loop — no pause/resume logic needed
- Update documentation to clarify ReadKey-based input pattern

The crash occurred when using Attack or any typed input during Live display.
ReadCommandInput already uses ReadKey correctly (fixed separately), but
ShowSkillTreeMenu's fallback path was still calling the broken PauseAndRun.

Closes #1265

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: update Barton history with PauseAndRun fix learnings

commit.message:
feat: initial template — Next.js SaaS AI Template

Production-ready Next.js boilerplate for building multi-tenant SaaS
products with AI built-in, published as a reusable template under
https://github.com/Create-Node-App/nextjs-saas-ai-template.

## Stack

- Next.js 15 (App Router, RSC, Turbopack) + TypeScript 5 (strict)
- PostgreSQL 17 + pgvector + Drizzle ORM
- Auth.js v5 + Auth0 (SSO) + database sessions
- OpenAI / Anthropic via Vercel AI SDK (RAG + embeddings)
- Tailwind CSS v4 + shadcn/ui
- AWS S3 / MinIO (local dev via DevContainer)
- next-intl i18n (EN + ES)
- Storybook + Jest + React Testing Library

## Architecture

- Multi-tenancy: tenant-scoped routes (/t/[tenant]), per-tenant DB
  isolation, TenantContext, tenant selection on login
- PBAC: roles are permission bundles; effective permissions = union of
  all roles; DB-resolved on every request
- Integration architecture: generic sync engine
  (integration_sync_control, integration_jobs) with GitHub OAuth2 as
  the included reference integration
- Outbound webhooks with delivery tracking and audit logging
- Admin panel: members, roles, invitations, settings, bulk import,
  webhook management
- Feature template (_feature-template_) for adding new features
- DevContainer: zero-config setup (app + PostgreSQL + MinIO)
- GitHub Actions: build, lint, type-check, tests, mega-linter

## Removed (from original private codebase)

- All HR domain features: skills, assessments, OKRs, learning, TRACK,
  People Finder, performance, 1:1 meetings, feedback, recognitions
- SST (Ion) infrastructure: sst.config.ts, sst-cron/, infra/, .sst/
- NanLabs deployment workflows (deploy.yml, pr-preview.yml)
- Cloud DB scripts (get-cloud-db-url.ts, cloud-db-setup.ts)
- All NanLabs / SkillsHouse branding and references
- Sensitive credentials (.env.production.local with live RDS password)
- HR integrations: BambooHR, Deel, Slack, Resource Guru, Small
  Improvements, LinkedIn, Gitlab, Lattice
- 33 HR database schemas and 25+ HR feature directories

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: fall back to HTTP when HTTPS probe fails during peer discovery

Some masternodes don't have TLS certificates configured on their RPC
port. The wallet was force-converting all endpoints to https://, so
those nodes failed the health check and were excluded — showing only
6/8 connections.

Changes:
- MasternodeClient::new() now preserves http:// if explicitly provided
  (only adds https:// prefix for bare hostnames with no scheme)
- Both the initial probe and the gossip probe now try https:// first;
  if the health check fails for any reason they retry with http://
- The working endpoint (http or https) is stored in PeerInfo so all
  subsequent RPC calls use the scheme that actually succeeded

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix hidapi include path on Windows and macOS

vcpkg (Windows) and brew (macOS) set the include root to the hidapi/
subdirectory itself, so the correct include is <hidapi.h> not
<hidapi/hidapi.h>. On Linux the system /usr/include is always in the
search path so both forms work, but <hidapi.h> is correct everywhere.

Fix BPM detection: detect peaks from same AC signal as waveform display

Previous detectBeat() used a separate EMA baseline that diverged from the
buffer-mean used for drawing, so beats visible on screen weren't detected.

New approach uses the same inverted AC = -(irValue - bufferMean) for both
waveform display and beat detection. Simple peak detector:
- Track when AC transitions from rising to falling (passed a peak)
- Only count as a beat if the peak is in the top 30% of the buffer range
- 333ms refractory period prevents double-counting
- BPM = 60000 / ms since last valid peak

If you can see peaks on the waveform, this will detect them.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[#202] Migrate workspace Python environment to uv (#324)

## Summary

Migrate workspace-level Python environment management to `uv` and align
local/devcontainer/CI workflows around a single `pyproject.toml` +
`uv.lock` source of truth.

This update also syncs the latest branch changes that address CI/review
follow-ups for uv setup behavior in GitHub Actions and Docker Ansible
provisioning.

## Changes

### Features
- Add workspace uv project metadata in `pyproject.toml`.
- Add committed lockfile `uv.lock` for reproducible dependency
resolution.
- Add helper script `scripts/uv-sync.sh` for workspace environment sync.
- Add Ansible role task file
`docker/ros/ansible/playbooks/roles/uv_setup/tasks/main.yml` for uv
installation in Docker image setup.

### Refactoring
- Update `.github/actions/setup-python-venv/action.yml` to use uv-aware
setup behavior and follow-up review fixes.
- Update devcontainer and compose bootstrap flow:
  - `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json`
  - `.devcontainer/docker-compose.yml`
- Update workspace settings in `Dr.QP.code-workspace` to align venv task
behavior.
- Update CI and Copilot workflows:
  - `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
  - `.github/workflows/copilot.yml`

### Documentation
- Update uv-based setup guidance in:
  - `docs/readme.md`
  - `docs/source/GettingStarted/installation.md`
  - `docs/source/notebooks/README`
- Update local Python instruction references:
  - `.github/instructions/python-local.instructions.md`
- Update skills docs to describe uv-first dependency workflow:
  - `.github/skills/ros2-dependency-management/SKILL.md`
  - `.github/skills/ros2-environment-setup/SKILL.md`

### Configuration
- Update `.gitignore` for venv-related ignore handling.
- Update Docker ROS setup playbook:
  - `docker/ros/ansible/playbooks/20_ros_setup.yml`

## Technical Details

### Approach
- Consolidate workspace Python dependency management into uv project
metadata plus lockfile.
- Keep developer workspace environment flow (`.venv`) explicit and
reproducible.
- Ensure CI and devcontainer automation use the same uv model as
contributor docs.

### Key File Coverage
- Core dependency model: `pyproject.toml`, `uv.lock`
- Automation/bootstrap: `.github/actions/setup-python-venv/action.yml`,
`.devcontainer/devcontainer.json`, `.devcontainer/docker-compose.yml`,
`scripts/uv-sync.sh`
- Docker provisioning:
`docker/ros/ansible/playbooks/roles/uv_setup/tasks/main.yml`
- Docs/instructions/skills: updated to reflect uv-first workflow

## Testing Strategy

### Automated
- Branch includes CI-related follow-up fixes and formatting/lint cleanup
commits.
- Workflow configuration updates were validated by existing PR checks
and review iteration commits.

### Manual
- Verified branch is synchronized:
  - merged latest `origin/main` state (already up to date)
  - fast-forwarded local branch to latest remote PR head
- Verified changed file set and stats against `origin/main..HEAD`.

## Breaking Changes

NO

### Migration Notes
- Use `uv sync` (or project helper tasks/scripts) to create/refresh
workspace `.venv`.
- Treat `pyproject.toml` and `uv.lock` as the source of truth for
workspace Python dependencies.
- Do not rely on legacy ad hoc requirements bootstrap for workspace
developer environment setup.

## Risk Assessment

### Risks
- Drift risk if future dependency updates change only one of docs, CI,
or devcontainer paths.
- Contributor confusion during transition from prior bootstrap habits.

### Mitigations
- Centralized uv metadata and lockfile committed in-repo.
- Updated CI/devcontainer/docs/skills in the same change set to keep
guidance consistent.

## Related
- Closes #202
- Related to #326

## Checklist
- [x] Workspace dependency management migrated to uv
- [x] Devcontainer/CI/workspace tasks aligned to uv workflow
- [x] Docker Ansible uv installation integrated
- [x] Documentation and skill instructions updated
- [x] Branch synchronized with remote PR head
